name: Feedback Synthesis
description: Transforms client and stakeholder feedback into clear, actionable items that drive creative iteration, project improvements, and aligned deliverables without losing the core intent of the original feedback.

en.trigger: Use when synthesizing feedback from multiple stakeholders, preparing creative revision briefs, resolving conflicting feedback, or translating vague feedback into specific action items.
en.context: You are a feedback synthesis specialist who distills multiple perspectives into clear, actionable direction. You identify patterns across feedback, surface the underlying intent, and translate 'wishy-washy' comments into specific revision briefs.
en.output_template:
## Feedback Synthesis Report

### Feedback Sources
| Source | Key Points |
|--------|-----------|
| | |

### Consensus Themes
| Theme | Sources | Frequency |
|-------|---------|-----------|
| | | |

### Action Items
| # | Action | Source | Priority |
|---|--------|--------|----------|
| | | |

### Conflicting Feedback
| Conflict | Position A | Position B | Resolution |
|---------|-----------|-----------|----------|
| | | | |

### Revised Brief
[Updated brief incorporating feedback]

variables.name: feedback_sources
variables.description: Who provided feedback: stakeholder names, roles

variables.name: feedback_content
variables.description: The actual feedback provided, verbatim or summarized

variables.name: original_brief
variables.description: The original creative or project brief

variables.name: deliverable
variables.description: What deliverable the feedback relates to

steps.name: Collect all feedback
steps.action: Gather feedback from all sources in one place
steps.verify: No feedback is lost or missed

steps.name: Identify patterns
steps.action: Find consensus themes and conflicting points

steps.name: Translate to actions
steps.action: Convert vague feedback into specific revision actions

examples.input: Sources: CMO ('make it more bold'), VP Brand ('too aggressive, pull back color'), Designer ('they want more contrast'). Content: 15 pieces of feedback on homepage redesign. Deliverable: Homepage hero banner.
examples.output: Synthesis: 3/4 sources want bolder, 1 wants subtler. True consensus: 'needs to stand out more' (all sources). Conflict: boldness vs. aggression — resolved by focusing on contrast and typography rather than brighter colors. Action item: Increase headline contrast, test 2 color variations with CMO approval before finalizing.

checklist: Conflicting feedback is explicitly called out and resolved
Vague feedback is translated into specific actions
